label: UCIMLR45_HD_CL
title: UCI ML Repository Cleveland Heart Disease Dataset
summary: Clinical non-invasive test data from 303 patients who underwent coronary angiography at the Cleveland Clinic (Cleveland, Ohio, USA). The dataset is commonly used to predict angiographic coronary artery disease status.
purpose: To support development and evaluation of probability algorithms for diagnosing coronary artery disease (CAD) using routinely available clinical factors and non-invasive test results, using coronary angiography as the reference standard.
baseline: Index time is the diagnostic evaluation leading to coronary angiography; measurements were recorded prior to (and without knowledge of) angiography results.
inclusionCriteria: Patients who underwent coronary angiography at the Cleveland Clinic without prior catheterisation or evidence of prior infarction or valve disease, and with coronary artery disease status unknown prior to angiography.
acquisitionMethods: Clinical and non-invasive test data collected during diagnostic evaluation for coronary angiography; specific acquisition protocols are not reported in source documentation.
limitations: Ethics approval and patient consent are not reported in the public dataset documentation. Detailed data acquisition and preprocessing protocols are not reported. This record is provided as a worked example based on the public UCI release. Note, this metadata example was generated by GPT-5.2 and may contain inaccuracies.

privacyCompliance: The UCI release is de-identified (patient names and direct identifiers removed or replaced); commonly used files contain no direct identifiers.
